title: Assistance for State, Local, and Tribal Governments
summary: This Treasury Department policy page provides informational descriptions of COVID-19 fiscal relief programs enacted under the American Rescue Plan (2021) and CARES Act (2020). The page has no publication date and appears to be an archival informational resource rather than a current policy announcement or market-relevant document.
key_points: American Rescue Plan provides $350 billion in emergency funding for eligible state, local, territorial, and Tribal governments
Capital Projects Fund (CCPF) addresses infrastructure needs including internet access in rural and low-income communities
Homeowner Assistance Fund: nearly $10 billion for states, territories, and Tribes to assist vulnerable homeowners
Emergency Rental Assistance Program: $21.6 billion for states, territories, and local governments to assist households unable to pay rent and utilities
State Small Business Credit Initiative: $10 billion for small business credit expansion initiatives
Coronavirus Relief Fund under CARES Act provides payments to state, local, and Tribal governments
why_it_matters: Historical fiscal policy documentation. This page describes pandemic-era government spending programs but does not announce new policy, provide updated program information, or contain market-relevant signals. It lacks temporal context to assess relevance to current market conditions.
macro_context: Describes fiscal support programs from 2020-2021 COVID-19 relief legislation - these are historical programs, not current macro catalysts.
important_numbers: $350 billion - American Rescue Plan emergency funding
$10 billion - State Small Business Credit Initiative
